content.text:
Question:
                                How many days ago did I watch the Super Bowl?

                                Question date:
                                2023/03/01 (Wed) 00:28

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                17 days ago. 18 days (including the last day) is also acceptable.

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:gpt4_af6db32f
            Context mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-9ae5b96d017b-claim-3 [ACTIVE] attended_event family=attended_event: The user watched the Super Bowl at a friend's house on 2023-02-12, where they had pizza and wings.; scope=User's dated event history; context="The user referenced this as inspiration while planning food for a fantasy football draft party."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:75853460f4fa017e04cf0bba39b31b19 quote="what I had at my friend's house today when we watched the Super Bowl - you know, pizza and wings"
- claim hot-path-9ae5b96d017b-claim-8 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wanted to use some of their $20 Super Bowl winnings to buy prizes for the fantasy football draft party.; scope=Fantasy football draft party planning discussed in the imported 2023-02-12 chat; context="The user asked for fun and creative prize ideas fitting for the party after mentioning the winnings."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:75853460f4fa017e04cf0bba39b31b19 quote="maybe I should use some of that money to get some prizes for the fantasy football draft party"
- claim hot-path-9ae5b96d017b-claim-6 [ACTIVE] current_metric_value family=current_metric_value: The user won $20 from a colleague after the Super Bowl.; scope=User's Super Bowl wager/winnings history; context="The user considered using the $20 Super Bowl winnings to buy prizes for the fantasy football draft party."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:75853460f4fa017e04cf0bba39b31b19 quote="I won $20 from my colleague after the Super Bowl"
- claim hot-path-9ae5b96d017b-claim-5 [ACTIVE] prefers family=prefers: The user supports the Kansas City Chiefs.; scope=User sports-team affinity; context="The user said “Go Chiefs!” in the context of watching the Super Bowl and planning football-related activities."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:75853460f4fa017e04cf0bba39b31b19 quote="Go Chiefs!"
- claim hot-path-9ae5b96d017b-claim-9 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user planned to get NFL team-branded hats, football-shaped snacks, and a “Worst Draft Pick” trophy as fantasy football draft party prizes.; scope=Fantasy football draft party planning discussed in the imported 2023-02-12 chat; context="The user selected a mix of football-themed items and humorous awards for the party prizes."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:75853460f4fa017e04cf0bba39b31b19 quote="Maybe I'll get a few NFL team-branded hats, some football-shaped snacks, and a "Worst Draft Pick" trophy."
- claim hot-path-9ae5b96d017b-claim-4 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user was planning a fantasy football draft party as of 2023-02-12.; scope=Fantasy football draft party planning discussed in the imported 2023-02-12 chat; context="The user asked for food, bar, TV content, decoration, and prize recommendations for the party."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:75853460f4fa017e04cf0bba39b31b19 quote="I'm trying to plan a fantasy football draft party"
- claim hot-path-9ae5b96d017b-claim-7 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user was considering holding the fantasy football draft party on a Sunday so they could catch up on NFL games before the draft.; scope=Fantasy football draft party planning discussed in the imported 2023-02-12 chat; context="The user asked for recommendations for sports bars showing NFL games in case friends wanted to catch a game before the party."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:75853460f4fa017e04cf0bba39b31b19 quote="I'm actually thinking of having the party on a Sunday, so I can catch up on some NFL games before the draft"

            Retrieved notes:
            - note hot-path-9ae5b96d017b-note-2 [PLAN/ACTIVE]: Fantasy football draft party prize plan; The user decided to buy a mix of football-themed items and humorous awards for a fantasy football draft party, specifically NFL team-branded hats, football-shaped snacks, and a "Worst Draft Pick" trophy, using part of their Super Bowl winnings.; scope=Planning context for the user's fantasy football draft party prizes; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:75853460f4fa017e04cf0bba39b31b19 quote="Maybe I'll get a few NFL team-branded hats, some football-shaped snacks, and a "Worst Draft Pick" trophy."

            Retrieved evidence:
            - source external:provided-text:75853460f4fa017e04cf0bba39b31b19 [external_record ref=longmemeval:gpt4_af6db32f:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/02/12 (Sun) 05:07
Transcript:
user: I'm trying to plan a fantasy football draft party and I was thinking of serving some food that's similar to what I had at my friend's house today when we watched the Super Bowl - you know, pizza and wings. Do you have any recommendations for good wing sauces or pizza toppings that would be a hit with my friends? By the way, Go Chiefs!...
...user: I'm actually thinking of having the party on a Sunday, so I can catch up on some NFL games before the draft. Do you have any recommendations for good sports bars in the area that show NFL games, in case some of my friends want to catch a game before the party?...
...user: I was thinking, since we're having a fantasy football draft party, it would be cool to have some TVs set up to show some football highlights or analysis before the draft. Do you have any recommendations for some good sports shows or channels that would get us pumped up for the draft?...
...* **NFL Fantasy Live**: A show dedicated to fantasy football, with expert advice, news, and updates....
...* **Fantasy Football Now**: A show that provides fantasy football advice, news, and updates....
...* **Sunday NFL Countdown** (ESPN): A pre-game show that airs on Sundays, featuring analysis and predictions from experts like Adam Schefter and Chris Mortensen.
* **Fantasy Football Today** (CBS Sports Network): A show that provides fantasy football advice, news, and updates, hosted by experts like Jamey Eisenberg and Dave Richard....
...* **Yahoo! Sports**: Features fantasy football advice, news, and updates, as well as live coverage of NFL games and analysis....
...user: I'm thinking of also having some sports-themed decorations to get everyone in the mood. Do you have any ideas for some simple yet effective decorations that would fit the theme?...
...user: I was thinking, since I won $20 from my colleague after the Super Bowl, maybe I should use some of that money to get some prizes for the fantasy football draft party. Do you have any ideas for some fun and creative prizes that would be fitting for the occasion?
assistant: What a great idea! Using your Super Bowl winnings to get prizes for the fantasy football draft party is a fantastic way to share the excitement and create a fun atmosphere. Here are some fun and creative prize ideas that are fitting for the occasion:...
...user: I think I'll get a few prizes that are a mix of football-themed items and humorous awards. Maybe I'll get a few NFL team-branded hats, some football-shaped snacks, and a "Worst Draft Pick" trophy. That way, everyone will have a good laugh and some fun prizes to look forward to. Thanks for the ideas!...
...If you need any more help or have any other questions, feel free to ask. Otherwise, I hope you and your friends have an amazing time at the draft party, and may the best team win!

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [CONCEPT]: Fantasy football draft party; Concept named Fantasy football draft party.
- entity entity:<1> [CONCEPT]: Super Bowl; Concept named Super Bowl.
- entity entity:<2> [ORGANIZATION]: Kansas City Chiefs; Organization named Kansas City Chiefs.
- entity entity:<3> [ORGANIZATION]: National Football League; Organization named National Football League.
- entity entity:<4> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.

user: I'm trying to plan a fantasy football draft party and I was thinking of serving some food that's similar to what I had at my friend's house today when we watched the Super Bowl - you know, pizza and wings.
